2603.22922.title: Quality Over Clicks: Intrinsic Quality-Driven Iterative Reinforcement Learning for Cold-Start E-Commerce Query Suggestion
2603.22922.paper_summary: Existing dialogue systems rely on Query Suggestion (QS) to enhance user engagement. Recent efforts typically employ large language models with Click-Through Rate (CTR) model, yet fail in cold-start scenarios due to their heavy reliance on abundant online click data for effective CTR model training. To bridge this gap, we propose Cold-EQS, an iterative reinforcement learning framework for Cold-Start E-commerce Query Suggestion (EQS). Specifically, we leverage answerability, factuality, and information gain as reward to continuously optimize the quality of suggested queries. To continuously optimize our QS model, we estimate uncertainty for grouped candidate suggested queries to select hard and ambiguous samples from online user queries lacking click signals. In addition, we provide an EQS-Benchmark comprising 16,949 online user queries for offline training and evaluation. Extensive offline and online experiments consistently demonstrate a strong positive correlation between online and offline effectiveness. Both offline and online experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our Cold-EQS, achieving a significant +6.81% improvement in online chatUV.
2603.22922.paper_summary_zh: 現有的對話系統依賴查詢建議（QS）來增強用戶參與度。最近的努力通常使用大型語言模型與點擊率（CTR）模型，但在冷啟動場景中失敗，因為它們過度依賴豐富的在線點擊數據來進行有效的CTR模型訓練。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了Cold-EQS，一個針對冷啟動電子商務查詢建議（EQS）的迭代強化學習框架。具體而言，我們利用可回答性、事實性和信息增益作為獎勵，持續優化建議查詢的質量。為了持續優化我們的QS模型，我們估計分組候選建議查詢的不確定性，以從缺乏點擊信號的在線用戶查詢中選擇困難和模糊的樣本。此外，我們提供了一個EQS基準，包括16,949個在線用戶查詢，用於離線訓練和評估。廣泛的離線和在線實驗一致顯示在線和離線效果之間存在強正相關性。離線和在線實驗結果均顯示我們的Cold-EQS的優越性，在在線chatUV中實現了顯著的+6.81%的提升。
2603.22922.author: Qi Sun et.al.
2603.22922.authors: Qi Sun, Kejun Xiao, Huaipeng Zhao, Tao Luo, Xiaoyi Zeng
